title: Dry-Tyre Legality State Machine
summary: FIA race regulations require two dry compounds in a dry race, while wet/intermediate conditions change that logic.
rationale: FIA race regulations require two dry compounds in a dry race, while wet/intermediate conditions change that logic. Current features count prior slicks, but not the full legal state: whether the driver still owes a compound change, whether the current compound repeats a previously used slick, and whether a wet-to-dry transition reactivates the dry-compound incentive.
implementation_hint: Build an online per driver-race state machine with OOF-safe features: used_dry_set, remaining_required_dry_count, current_compound_already_used, stop_debt_if_dry, wet_exemption_flag, and laps_remaining_when_still_illegal. Interact these with LapsRemaining_Est, Compound, and Is_WetWeather; train as a small specialist or add to the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Should improve mid/late-race pit calls where the car can technically finish but still has a regulation-driven reason to stop.
risk: If many rows come from wet or mixed-condition sessions, a naive legality feature can misfire unless wet exemptions are modeled explicitly and leakage-safe race conditions are inferred only from observed compounds.
